sourceMovie.title: John Wick
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit John Wick for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Chad Stahelski keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If John Wick clicked for you, John Wick: Chapter 2 (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If John Wick clicked for you, John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Nobody
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If John Wick clicked for you, Nobody (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after John Wick?
faq.answer: Try John Wick: Chapter 2, John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum, Nobody—each shares DNA with John Wick while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is John Wick?
faq.answer: John Wick (2014) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is John Wick part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "John Wick Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
